description_long: This object consists of three distinct guard rails, each with a different design. The leftmost guard rail features a solid rectangular panel with angled metal supports and a horizontal wooden plank at the base. The center guard rail has a flat, rectangular metal panel with a small yellow reflective plate in the middle, supported by two vertical posts. The rightmost guard rail is constructed from a series of evenly spaced vertical metal bars connected by two horizontal rails, forming a classic crowd-control barrier. All three are made primarily of metal, with some wood and plastic elements visible. The surfaces are painted in muted tones, with some signs of wear and utilitarian design. These guard rails are typically used to block, guide, or protect pedestrians and vehicles in urban environments.
description: A set of three urban guard rails: one with a solid panel and wood base, one with a flat metal panel and yellow reflector, and one with vertical metal bars.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three guard rails side by side, highlighting their distinct designs and structural details.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profiles of the guard rails, emphasizing their thickness and support legs.
description_view_2: Rear view of the guard rails, revealing the back structure and support elements.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profiles and the arrangement of the support legs.
